Sr. No. Description UNIT Total Qty
E CHILLED WATER PIPING & VALVES:
Supply, installation, testing & commissioning of thermally insulated MS Class C (heavy) chilled water piping
as per IS1239 / IS 3589 (8mm pipe thickness) complete with bends, elbows, reducers all fittings (provision
to install sensor of BMS) & supports as required as per enclosed specification. anti microbial insulation for
chilled water piping. The insulation shall be closed cell nitrile rubber insulation class O density 48kg/m3,
covered with canvass & vapour barrier shall be 18 oz. teflon fiberglass cloth/covered with canvass & vapour
barrier Foster compound / Lag coating+cora cloth. Insulation shall be fixed with adhesive as per
specifications. 32 mm thick insulation for Pipe size upto 125mm dia. and 50 mm thick for pipe size above
1 125mm diameter. Multi layer insulation shall be with staggered seams. Final finished colour of coating will be
as per approved shade. (Inside Piping)
Insulation color should be as per following :
- Internal Piping
- CHWS: Blue
- CHWR: Gray
- External Piping
- CHWS & CHWR: Black w/ Aluminum Cladding

files.text:
DOCUMENT NO: ACXNMI01-KDC-DC-ZZ-SPC-ME-0006
be fully factory (machine) slit along each length with two strips of double-sided self-adhesive
tape pre-applied to each open slit surface. The self-tape adhesive shall be solvent free acrylic
with high initial tack and high peel strength. For pipe dia. 4 inches & above, sheet insulation
should be used.
2.17.3. Duct Insulation Installation
Sheet metal ducts shall be insulated as per method given below:
 Duct surfaces shall be cleaned to remove all grease, oil, dirt, etc. prior to carrying out
insulation work.
 Measurement of surface dimensions shall be taken properly to cut closed cell elastomeric
rubbers sheets to size with sufficient allowance in dimension.
 Material shall be fitted under compression, and no stretching of material should be
allowed.
 A thin film of adhesive (Pidilite SR 505 / Insulation manufacturer recommended adhesive)
shall be applied on the back of the insulating material sheet and then on to the metal
surface.
 When adhesive is tack dry, insulating material sheet shall be placed in position and
pressed firmly to achieve a good bond.
 All longitudinal and transverse joints shall be sealed as per manufacturer
recommendations.
 All joints will be covered with overlapping self-adhesive tape 50 mm wide, procured from
manufacture of same material as the base insulation. . (All corners shall be sealed with 50
mm wide self-adhesive aluminum foil).
 Fix PVC strapping 25 mm wide at 600 mm intervals.
2.17.4. PIPING INSULATION
The thickness of piping insulation for various pipe sizes operating under different temperature
ranges shall be as given in bill of quantities.
METHOD: APPLICATION FOR NITRILE RUBBER INSULATION:
 Brush and clean the pipes to ensure removal of rust, dust, grease, etc.
 Apply minimum 2 coats of zinc chromate primer.
 Apply thin film of adhesive (Pidilite SR 998 / Insulation manufacturer recommended
adhesive) and leave it 2 – 3 min. to let it become tacky.
 Press the sheets in portion and butt the joints well together.
 Apply 75 mm self-adhesive tape of same material on both type of joints (longitudinal and
transverse direction). Tape shall be min. 2.5 mm thick.
 Finishing on Pipe insulation shall be provided with 24 G Aluminum cladding.
 For outdoor application, provide weatherproof coating on the surface of insulation as per
manufacturer recommendation & as recommended in the BOQ.
TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR
REV – R1 PAGE | 66
MECHANICAL LOWSIDE

DOCUMENT NO: ACXNMI01-KDC-DC-ZZ-SPC-ME-0006
2.18.17. ACOUSTIC INSULATION
 Material shall be engineered Nitrile Rubber open cell foam.
 The Random Incidence Sound Absorption Coefficient (RISAC); tested as per ISO 354.
 The material should be fibre free.
 The density of the same shall be within 140-180 Kg/m3.
 It should have Microban®*; antimicrobial product protection and should pass Fungi
Resistance as per ASTM fi 21 and Bacterial Resistance as per ASTM E 2180.
 The material should have a thermal conductivity not exceeding 0.047 W/m.K @ 20ºC
 The material should withstand maximum surface temperature of +85 Deg.C and minimum
surface temperature of -20 Deg.C
 The insulation should pass Air Erosion Resistance Test in accordance to ASTM Standard C
1071-05 (section 12.7)
PROCEDURE:
The inside surface for the ducts shall be covered with adhesive recommended by the
manufacturer. Cut Foamed sheets into required sizes apply adhesive on the foam and stick it
to the duct surface.
2.19. NOISE AND VIBRATION CONTROL
2.19.1. SCOPE
The scope of these specifications comprises of the supply & erection of noise and vibration
isolation equipment as called for in the Bill of quantities and Equipment schedule.
2.19.2. GENERAL SPECIFICATIONS
 All vibration control equipment shall meet the intent of specifications.
 Any request for changes or modifications in the specifications must be submitted in
writing in time for review, approval and issuance through a written addendum to the
specifications, prior to price bid opening.
 The contractor shall be responsible for verifying the completeness of the isolation
requirements and the correctness and overall suitability of the equipment to meet the
intent of specifications. Equipment needed to meet these requirements, even if,
specifically not mentioned herein or shown on the drawings, shall be supplied by the
contractor without claim for additional payment.
 The minimum static deflection of each vibration isolation unit and unit type and unit base
type shall be as shown in the equipment schedule or as specifically required.
TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR
REV – R1 PAGE | 77
MECHANICAL LOWSIDE
